description: Long-lived static cloud access keys embedded in environment variables, images, or config instead of using a managed identity and secrets store.
body: Failure mode: a workload authenticates with long-lived access keys baked into env vars or an image, so the keys never rotate, appear in logs and snapshots, and grant standing access if leaked. Detection signals: secret scanners find access keys in images/config; credentials lack rotation; the same key is reused across environments. Do not relocate the key to another plaintext store as a fix. Safer fallback: use a workload/managed identity or fetch short-lived credentials from the secrets manager with rotation, and scope access per identity. Risk boundary: rotate any exposed key immediately and escalate the disclosure rather than leaving it in place.
